system.description: Take any number of things you’ve Borrowed or Stolen and place them together with any number of objects. Say “It’s alive!” and roll [quality].

outcomes.success: you create a person from the pieces. The pieces used to create them become their only qualities, at first. The rest of their details are filled out smoothly with a “perfectly normal person,” as defined by your perspective. They regard you as a creator and generally pleasantly, though their tendency toward existential discomfort will depend on the things used to create them. You lose the Borrowed or Stolen things when you do this.
outcomes.every3rd3: you can stretch your materials to make an additional person from the same parts, identical or slightly modified.
outcomes.failure: one of the pieces will overtake the “perfectly normal person” with all the details of its source. The person you create is the person that piece was taken from, except for the other included pieces.
